Package global.thalion.ttio

Class AcquisitionRun

java.lang.Object
global.thalion.ttio.AcquisitionRun
All Implemented Interfaces:
Encryptable, Indexable<Spectrum>, Provenanceable, Run, Streamable<Spectrum>, AutoCloseable
public class AcquisitionRun extends Object implements Indexable<Spectrum>, Streamable<Spectrum>, Provenanceable, Encryptable, Run, AutoCloseable
An ordered collection of spectra from one acquisition, with instrument config, spectrum index, and optional chromatograms.

HDF5 layout: /study/ms_runs/<name>/ with subgroups spectrum_index/, signal_channels/, instrument_config/, chromatograms/ (optional), and provenance/ (optional).

Conforms to Indexable, Streamable, and Provenanceable. Encryptable conformance is deferred to M41.5.

v0.7 M44: I/O routed through StorageGroup / StorageDataset; this class no longer references the low-level Hdf5Group / Hdf5Dataset types.

API status: Stable (Encryptable surface pending).

Cross-language equivalents: Objective-C TTIOAcquisitionRun, Python ttio.acquisition_run.AcquisitionRun.

      public int count()
      Description copied from interface: Run
      Number of measurements in the run. Modality-specific: spectra for AcquisitionRun, aligned reads for GenomicRun.

      count in interface Indexable<Spectrum>
      Specified by:
      count in interface Run
      Returns:
      the total number of elements.

    • get

      public Object get(int index)
      Phase 1: modality-agnostic accessor required by Run. Delegates to objectAtIndex(int); the typed return is widened to Object so callers iterating uniformly over AcquisitionRun + GenomicRun see a single signature.
      Specified by:
      get in interface Run

    • encryptWithKey

      public void encryptWithKey(byte[] key, Enums.EncryptionLevel level) throws Exception
      Description copied from interface: Encryptable
      Encrypt this object's protectable content at the given granularity.
      Specified by:
      encryptWithKey in interface Encryptable
      Parameters:
      key - raw key material; length and format are algorithm-specific
      level - the granularity at which to apply protection
      Throws:
      Exception - if encryption fails (key material invalid, I/O error, etc.)

    • decryptWithKey

      public void decryptWithKey(byte[] key) throws Exception
      Description copied from interface: Encryptable
      Decrypt previously-encrypted content.
      Specified by:
      decryptWithKey in interface Encryptable
      Parameters:
      key - raw key material matching the key used to encrypt
      Throws:
      Exception - if decryption fails (wrong key, data corrupt, etc.)
